My role — revenue ownership

I led a team of 5 at Mesa — P&G's official distributor in Vietnam. My job was simple: own the total GMV number. I tracked 5 traffic sources daily, found which one was falling behind, and pushed the right person to act. The one thing I did hands-on myself was Live affiliate — booking KOLs, negotiating commissions, and managing creator relationships directly.

Role
Store Owner — P&G Beauty on TikTok Shop, through Mesa (P&G's official distributor in Vietnam)
How I lead the team to achieve target together
Achievement

Mid-month target gap — Olay SBD campaign

Closed a mid-month GMV gap by negotiating more Mega Live slots with a Top Creator myself.
Result: 121% of base target (98% of stretch target) over the 3-day campaign — ranked #1 nationally in the Beauty category on TikTok Shop.

Daily GMV trend for the campaign month — the peak at Mar 11–13 aligns with the Olay SBD push.

Daily GMV trend for the campaign month — the peak aligns with the SBD push above.

Team recap message for the campaign — colleague names blurred for privacy.

TikTok Shop Operations Process

This is a framework I built myself, after tracking and coordinating 5 sales channels on TikTok Shop. It is not an internal document from any company — it's how I organized my own knowledge to apply in future roles.

Live inhouse
Live affiliate
Video inhouse
Video affiliate
Product card

Full step-by-step detail for each channel is in the Standard Operating Procedures section below.

Standard operating procedures

One GMV target, five traffic sources. Here's the full picture — tap any card below to see how I run that source, with a real example and result.

SOP — Live inhouse (18h / show)

Role: Khoa owns the GMV target + deal structure for this source. The host team is the key person who runs it, based on the brief Khoa approves.
Khoa (Owner)
Key person (does the task)

7-step script funnel — the core of every live show

01
Hook / FOMOGet attention right away — viral angle, social proof, urgency
02
Dramatize problemMake the customer's problem feel bigger and more urgent
03
USP solutionShow the solution — what makes the product different
04
Prove resultLive demo/test, before-after, real proof on screen
05
Create demandBuild long-term demand — not just one purchase, but a habit
06
MinigameKeep viewers engaged before the close
07
CTA closeA clear call to action, tied to a specific deal/voucher
Planning
Khoa
Set the GMV/session target based on the monthly breakdown. Approve the deal structure: flash deals for peak hours (12pm, 8–10pm)
Planning
Key (Host)
Check equipment: camera, mic, lighting, internet, live software
Planning
Key (Host)
Build the script using the approved 7-step funnel, learn deals + USP, set up products on screen
Execution
Key (Host)
Follow all 7 funnel steps in order, do not skip any
Execution
Khoa
Check dashboard every 30 minutes: GMV, viewers, CVR by funnel stage
Execution
Khoa
If CVR is low or viewers drop >20% → push the host to adjust the deal/script live on air
Optimize
Khoa
Compare real GMV vs target at each funnel step — find the weak stage to improve next time

Funnel-stage debug

Drop at HookChange the FOMO angle, try a different social proof
Drop at USPMake the difference clearer, use a direct comparison
Low CVR at CTAAdd more minigames, build stronger urgency
Viewers drop mid-showAdjust the concept based on real-time feedback, push a new flash deal

Example

CVR dropped sharply right after the Hook segment in one show. Checked the funnel-stage debug table, swapped the FOMO angle mid-show, and added a stronger social-proof clip.

Result

CVR recovered within the same session — confirmed the Hook was the weak stage, not the offer itself.

SOP — Live affiliate

Role: This is the source Khoa is most hands-on with — booking creators myself, negotiating deals, building KOL relationships, and owning the final ROI.

Channel mix strategy — 3 sources at once

Solo booking

Mega Live (Top Creator) and Daily Live (KOC) — I choose each creator myself based on the brief

MCN network

Connect with MCNs to add more live shows, and get better help negotiating deals/commission

Exclusive booking

Book exclusive live shows with Top Creators at the same time → keeps a stable base of GMV

Planning
Khoa
Tier KOLs: Mega Live vs Daily Live. Book myself by schedule + slot, brief on USP, price floor, key message
Planning
Khoa
Negotiate commission tiers based on NMV — set a separate affiliate link for each KOL to track accurately
Execution
Key (Creator)
Post a teaser video before the live show to build anticipation
Execution
Khoa
Watch the live show: check the KOL is using the right deal, price, brief. Track NMV in real time via affiliate link
Execution
Khoa
Help with hooks/deals if a KOL is underperforming in the first hour. Make sure stock matches the forecast
Ads handoff flow
Creator sends video code + ads code → Khoa passes it to the ads team → Ads team boosts the live show
Optimize
Khoa
Calculate real ROI = NMV / (fee + commission + ads spend). Compare organic GMV vs GMV with ads boost
Report
Khoa
Rank: Top / Maintain / Cut. Top → upgrade to exclusive commission. Update the KOL database

Example

Mid-campaign, GMV was behind target with 10 days left. Negotiated more Mega Live slots with a Top Creator myself to close the gap.

Result

121% of base target over the 3-day campaign — ranked #1 nationally in Beauty (full story in the Achievement section above).

SOP — Video inhouse

Content taxonomy — 4 fixed types

Introduction

First-time intro of a product or brand

Entertainment

Fun content tied to the brand, easy to go viral

Review

Feature review, real demo

Engagement

Community interaction, react to trends

Role: The agency content team is the key person who produces the videos. Khoa reviews, approves concepts, and tracks performance to decide what's next.
Planning
Key (Agency)
Build a content calendar with 5 fixed series, tied to the content taxonomy. Use mascot/character storytelling when it fits the brand
Planning
Key (Agency)
Write a detailed script: opening/middle/ending scenes, setting, music, props
Execution
Key (Agency)
Film using the detailed script, follow the agreed setting and brand cues. Edit with the standard brand template
Layer 1 — Before filming

Khoa gives feedback on the proposed angle/hook — adjust the concept before official production

Layer 2 — After editing

Khoa gives feedback on the draft video — check brand cues and pace before posting

Execution
Khoa
Review and approve before posting. Approve the upload schedule for peak hours
Optimize
Khoa
Track performance 4 days after posting — compare to target, give feedback to the agency if needed
Report
Khoa
Review the agency's weekly A/B hook test — pick the winner to guide the next series

Example

A Review-type video underperformed its view target by day 4. Used the weekly A/B hook test to try a different opening angle for the next post in that series.

Result

The new hook outperformed the original and became the template for the rest of that content series.

SOP — Video affiliate (seeding)

Role: The ops team is the key person doing daily seeding. Khoa researches the initial strategy, tracks GMV to find winners, and decides when to double down with ads.
Kalodata research MCN network AM TikTok Shop GMV Max
Planning
Khoa
Research KOCs with a data tool: real GMV, conversion rate, product category — never just follower count
Planning
Khoa
Build the seeding list by tier: Celeb / Top KOL / KOC. Connect through 3 sources: my own outreach, MCN, AM TikTok Shop
Execution
Key (Ops)
Send sample + brief, confirm it arrived. Remind after 7 days, flag after 14 days if not posted
Execution
Key (Ops)
Quality check: brief, product, compliance
Execution
Khoa
Track GMV for each video with its own tracking link — find the videos with strong sales
Optimize
Khoa
Rank after 30 days: Star / Maintain / Sleep / Cut. Winning video → ask for or buy the ads code from the creator
Double-down flow
Find the winning video → Get or buy the ads code → Send to ads team → Boost the video → Push into GMV Max → Auto-delivered to a wider audience
Report
Khoa
Update the KOC master database. Compare GMV uplift after boost vs the starting point

Example

A KOC video posted through seeding started showing strong GMV within the first few days. Asked the creator for the ads code and pushed it into GMV Max.

Result

GMV for that video scaled further after the ads boost, confirmed by comparing organic vs. boosted GMV.

SOP — Product card

Role: Khoa sets the SKU strategy and pricing direction. The ads team is the key person who sets up ads + PSA based on the list Khoa sends.
Planning
Khoa
Group SKUs: A (hero, push hard) / B (volume) / C (long-tail). Set the pricing strategy
Planning
Khoa
Audit the listing: does the title have the main keyword? Are the photos the right size? Is the product video under 60s?
Execution
Key (Listing team)
Set up a clean listing: keyword in the first 15 words of the title, 6–8 high-quality photos
Execution
Key (Ads team)
Run Product Shopping Ads for A-tier SKUs: CPA target, do not raise the bid too fast in the first 3 days
Execution
Khoa
Compile the month's Top Selling SKU list — send it to the ads team
PSA handoff flow
Khoa compiles the month's Top Selling SKUs → Sends the list to ads team → Ads team runs PSA → SKU rises in search
Optimize
Khoa
Debug by funnel: low impression → keyword/ads issue. Low CTR → photo/title. Low CVR → price/reviews

Funnel debug

Low impressionKeyword/ads bid issue — re-research keywords + raise bid
Low CTR despite high impressionPhotos/title not appealing — A/B test the thumbnail
Low CVR despite good CTRPrice not competitive, or weak reviews
ROAS dropping over timeAd fatigue — refresh the creative every 2–3 weeks
Report
Khoa
Decide to cut ads if ROAS < 1 after 7 days. Compare search ranking before/after PSA

Example

A hero SKU had high impressions but low CTR. Debug table pointed to photos/title, so ran an A/B test on the thumbnail.

Result

CTR improved after the new photos, and the SKU moved onto the month's Top Selling list, which then got PSA support.

Case study
P&G Beauty · TikTok Shop Vietnam · 2024–2025

Scaling from a low baseline to +9,300% GMV by coordinating 5 traffic sources at once

+9,300%
GMV growth
from a near-zero baseline
#1
National rank, Beauty
3-day SBD campaign
5/5
Sources tracked
Store Owner
Role

Context

P&G Beauty entered TikTok Shop Vietnam with low GMV while beauty competitors had already invested heavily in livestream and affiliate. As Store Owner through a distributor, my role was to own the total GMV target and the deal list — not to produce content or host shows myself, but to coordinate the key people in charge of each source to reach the shared goal.

Recognition

P&G Beauty appeared in TikTok Shop's official top-seller rankings for the Beauty category across three major sale events.

11.11 — Top 5
12.12 — Top 3
3.3 — Top 5

Problem

  • Only 1–2 sources were active, so livestream and affiliate power was not used
  • No ROI tracking system per channel — no clear sense of where to put the budget
  • KOL/KOC seeding was based on gut feeling, with no pipeline to sort winners from losers

Action

01
Break down the target by source Split the total GMV target across 5 sources based on expected contribution — the base for weekly tracking and diagnosis.
02
Book and negotiate Live affiliate myself Negotiated directly with KOLs/KOCs, mixing solo booking + MCN network + exclusive booking to keep a stable base of GMV.
03
Track and approve content from the agency Approved concepts for Live inhouse and Video inhouse before production, reviewed performance to guide what comes next.
04
Diagnose and push the right person when target is missed When GMV fell short of target, found which source was weakest, and pushed the right person to take clear action (more bookings, new deals, boost ads with PSA).
05
Turned it into an Operations Map From tracking each source closely, I built a full operations framework — a clear view of Planning/Execution/Optimization/Reporting across all 5 traffic sources.

Result

GMV growth vs baseline +9,300%
Traffic sources tracked 5 / 5
SBD campaign result (Olay) 121% of base target · #1 nationally
Repeat national ranking Top 3–6, three major sale events
NAD campaign GMV Brand's highest ever, 2.6–3x uplift
Operations framework Built, transferable

Team recap message for the NAD campaign — colleague names blurred for privacy.

Learning

An owner doesn't need to do everything, just know who to push

The value is not in doing every task yourself — it's in reading the numbers, finding the real problem, and pushing the right person at the right time.

Tracking is the foundation for scaling

Without breaking down the target by source, every push is a guess. Track each source closely from day one.

Direct relationships are hard to copy

The one part I did myself — KOL relationships in Live affiliate — turned out to be the hardest part of the whole system to replace.

Stanhome · Shopee Mall · 2023–2024

From 20–30 orders/month to 100+ orders/month on Shopee — through SEO listing, ad optimization, and a data-driven SKU strategy

+233%
Orders/month
9.47
Shopee ads ROAS
+741%
Top SKU revenue
Top 10
Shopee search · 2 keywords
Hands-on
Full executor

Context

A different role from P&G: at Stanhome, I did everything myself — no key person in between. Stanhome is a French pharmacy cosmetics brand with 60 years of history, on Shopee Mall Vietnam but with low GMV — only 20–30 products sold per month. There was no real listing strategy, ads were not optimized, and there was no SKU grouping strategy. I did the SEO listing, set up ads, built an analytics dashboard, and planned the budget across channels myself.

Problem

  • Listings had no main keyword — products did not show up in organic search
  • Ads ran with no SKU data to guide them — budget was spread evenly instead of focused on strong products
  • No system to analyze traffic by channel (Shopee Key, Google, Facebook) to know which one was converting
  • Shop page was not optimized — low CTR, high bounce rate

Action

01
SEO listing for the whole catalog Researched keywords for each SKU, optimized titles to put the main keyword in the first 15 words. Result: 2 key products (moisturizer + body wash) reached the top 10 in Shopee search for their category keyword.
02
Group SKUs by traffic data + conversion rate Built a dashboard to track traffic by source (Shopee Key, Google+Google, Key+Google+Facebook). Sorted into 4 groups: high traffic/high CR → keep as is; high traffic/low CR → optimize ads; low traffic/high CR → raise budget; low traffic/low CR → fix listing + creative.
03
Optimized CPC ads with controlled budget Set up CPC ads for priority SKUs, tracked CTR + ROAS every week. Scaled budget 20% at a time for winning SKUs. Result: shop-wide ROAS reached 9.47 on a 3-month spend.
04
Built a multi-channel budget plan Built a spending plan file by channel (Shopee, Lazada, Facebook, Google, TikTok, Zalo, Tiki) with revenue forecast and cost/revenue ratio per channel — used by management to decide the monthly budget split.
05
Sent regular reports with an optimization loop Sent monthly reports with clear recommendations by SKU group — not just numbers, but the next action to take. In June: Balance Shampoo sales doubled vs May, Soft Shower grew 50%.

Result

Orders/month 20–30 → 100+ (+233%)
Shop-wide ROAS (3 months) 9.47
Moisturizer SKU revenue +535.6% vs previous period
Balance Shampoo SKU revenue +741.3% · CTR up +44.6%
SEO listing Top 10 Shopee · 2 category keywords

Proof

SEO top 10 — moisturizer

SEO top 10 — shower gel

Push sale — before (T6.2023) vs after (T8.2024)

Orders grew from 20–30 to 100+ per month

Data analyst dashboard — SKUs grouped by traffic × CR

Ads ROAS 9.47 · Doanh thu SKU +741%

Learning

SEO listing is the foundation, not an option

You can turn ads off anytime, but an optimized listing keeps bringing organic traffic forever. Do SEO first, then scale ads — not the other way around.

Group SKUs before spending on ads

Spreading the budget equally across all SKUs is the fastest way to waste money. Group SKUs by traffic × CR to know exactly where to invest.

A report with recommendations beats a report with numbers

A report with only numbers is just a dashboard. The real value is reading the numbers and giving the next action right in the report.
